name: docs_export
purpose: Generate a standalone Node.js script (scripts/exportDocs.js) that exports all viewer documents to MkDocs or Confluence. The script calls the running viewer's REST API to fetch the document tree, templates, and content â€” then renders each doc through its Mustache template and writes output files.

viewerApi.note: Live viewer REST API â€” must be running at config.export.viewerUrl

endpoints./api/catalog: document-catalog.json â€” docType â†’ templateRef mapping

script_generation.runtime: Node.js CommonJS â€” no ESM, no TypeScript, no build step
viewer_api_usage: GET /api/tree    â†’ full doc tree (sections[]{ label, docs[], groups[] })
GET /api/catalog â†’ { document_types: [{ id, templateRef, format }] }
GET /api/template/{name} â†’ raw Mustache template string
rendering_pipeline: If doc has no file â†’ skip (virtual doc)
If docType is in SKIP_TYPES (source-code, ui-source-code, sql, schema-state, sample-data) â†’ skip
If ext === 'md' â†’ use content as-is
If ext === 'json' AND catalogEntry.templateRef exists â†’ normalize data + Mustache.render â†’ Markdown
Otherwise â†’ wrap raw content in fenced code block

normalizers_required.ui-module-spec: pages[] â†’ add roles, requirements, menuVisibleLabel, hasActions, hasFilters, hasValidation booleans
normalizers_required.mde-command: flatten requires/produces objects to key-value lists
normalizers_required.mde-skill: flatten inputs/outputs objects to key-value lists
normalizers_required.all others: pass data through unchanged â€” template handles it

mkdocs_output.index: output/export/mkdocs/docs/index.md â€” site name + generated date

yml.nav_format:
- SectionLabel:
  - DocLabel: section/doc.md
  - GroupLabel:
    - DocLabel: section/group/doc.md

confluence_output.body_format: { type:'page', title, space:{key}, ancestors:[{id:parentId}], body:{storage:{value:html, representation:'storage'}} }
confluence_output.html_conversion: Convert rendered Markdown to HTML using marked npm package
confluence_output.hierarchy: One root page per export run, one section page per tree section, one content page per doc
confluence_output.requires_config: config.export.confluence: baseUrl, spaceKey, email, apiToken â€” abort with clear message if missing

error_handling: If viewer is unreachable â†’ abort with: 'Cannot reach viewer at {url} â€” is it running?'
If a doc fails to render â†’ log [skip] docLabel: reason, continue
If Confluence page creation fails â†’ log [fail] title: error, continue
Print per-doc [ok]/[skip]/[fail] lines for CI visibility

rules: CRITICAL: Script must be pure CommonJS â€” no import/export, no TypeScript.
CRITICAL: Use the viewer's /api/tree and /api/catalog to discover all docs â€” never hardcode paths.
CRITICAL: Patch package.json by reading and merging â€” never overwrite.
CRITICAL: Patch configuration.json by reading and merging â€” never overwrite existing keys.
CRITICAL: Confluence auth credentials go in configuration.json with empty defaults â€” never hardcode tokens.
Template rendering must use the mustache npm package (require('mustache')).
Confluence HTML conversion must use the marked npm package (require('marked')).
Screenshots are copied from config.screenshots.outputDir to docs/assets/screenshots/.
mkdocs.yml nav must mirror the tree hierarchy from /api/tree.
Skipped doc types: source-code, ui-source-code, sql, schema-state, sample-data â€” these are too verbose for documentation.
